Subject: return for order 48213 - flagging for you
Date: Wed, 5 Mar 2025 15:40

Dave,

Got the jacket back for 48213 today. Tags are still on, doesn't look worn,
smells fine (we do actually check that lol). So this one's a normal
approve -- I updated the Returns Tracker row to "Inspected - Approved" and
picked "reship correct size" since that's what the customer wanted per her
original ticket.

Question though -- I logged the reship request but I don't have a way to
actually trigger the new shipment myself, I think that still needs someone
on your side to create the replacement order in Order Mgmt System? Can you
do that or should I ping ops-lead directly? We should probably fix this
handoff at some point, I keep having to escalate every single reship.

Separately: got ANOTHER return in yesterday (order #48390) that's the
opposite case -- item's clearly been worn (deodorant marks, no tags) and
customer wants a refund. Per policy that's a deny -- can't restock a worn
item, no refund, no reship. I marked it "Inspected - Denied" in the
tracker and I think we're supposed to email the customer with the reason?
Not sure who normally sends that email, want to confirm with you before I
just delete the ticket.

- Priya, Warehouse
